title: US cable on DeepSeek meets a CRISPR cure and orbital solar - TCR 04/27/26
description:
The U.S. State Department directs diplomats on six continents to confront foreign governments over alleged AI distillation by DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax, China blocks Meta's acquisition of Manus AI, and Intellia's Phase 3 in-vivo CRISPR trial cuts hereditary angioedema attacks by 87% from a single infusion.
This episode covers the State Department distillation cable, China's Manus blockade, Intellia's single-infusion gene-editing breakthrough, Meta's space-based solar agreement with Overview Energy, Vietnam importing an entire AI stack from SK Group, and Tesla's $114 billion share registration for Elon Musk.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-27-2026/

0:00 State Department distillation cable to six continents
0:40 Vietnam imports SK Group's full AI stack
9:36 Meta's orbital solar agreement with Overview Energy
14:58 Tesla's $114 billion share registration
16:49 Close

The State Department cable instructs U.S. diplomatic posts worldwide to warn allied governments about Chinese AI distillation, naming DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax specifically - the same week DeepSeek V4 crossed 1.6 trillion open-weight parameters running on domestic Huawei silicon. Forty-eight hours later, China announced it would block Meta's acquisition of Manus AI, the most aggressive state action yet against AI talent migration to the United States. Intellia Therapeutics reported Phase 3 data showing 87% attack reduction and 62% of patients attack-free six months after a single in-vivo CRISPR infusion - the first Phase 3 result for any in-vivo gene-editing therapy targeting a disease-causing gene. Meta signed a capacity reservation agreement with Overview Energy for up to 1 GW of solar power generated in geosynchronous orbit and beamed via infrared light to terrestrial solar farms, with 1,000 satellites planned beginning 2030. Vietnam signed two MoUs with SK Group to import a "Korean-style AI full-stack" spanning semiconductors, energy, data centers, models, and services - the first overseas expansion of an integrated sovereign AI offering. Tesla filed paperwork to deliver 303,960,630 shares to Elon Musk under his 2018 pay package, ending a six-year legal saga while the same board approved a follow-on package potentially worth ten times more.
